_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Robust Chromium resolution for dashboard PDF / Mermaid rendering in containers

steps.description: Add _resolve_chromium_binary() (env IW_PLAYWRIGHT_CHROME_PATH -> newest ~/.cache/ms-playwright/chromium-*/chrome -> shutil.which chromium/chromium-browser/google-chrome/google-chrome-stable -> None) to dashboard/utils/markdown.py; route render_pdf_chromium() and _render_mermaid_mmdc() through it; keep the existing IW_PLAYWRIGHT_CHROME_PATH env name (do not add a new one); keep the graceful None behavior
Code review of S01 (resolver priority order, no DB import added, graceful-None path preserved)

Provision a Chromium browser in Dockerfile.e2e (apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends chromium + minimal headless runtime libs, cleaned in the same RUN layer); do not remove the existing libpango deps; the per-worktree compose app container is out of scope (see design Notes)

Code review of S03 (Dockerfile.e2e change minimal and correct; --no-install-recommends + cleanup in same layer; libpango deps retained; no docker lifecycle commands; non-root + --no-sandbox compatibility; image still starts the dashboard the same way)

Unit tests for _resolve_chromium_binary(): env override wins; non-existent env path ignored; newest chromium-* glob picked; shutil.which fallback; None -> graceful (tests/dashboard/test_markdown_chromium.py). Update tests/dashboard/test_docs_pdf_chromium.py only if it asserts the old hardcoded path
Code review of S05 (resolver test coverage; isolation via monkeypatch/tmp_path; no live DB import)
Final cross-agent review: resolver correctness + priority order, image provisioning sane, graceful degradation intact, AC1-AC5 traced
QV: Browser verification — /pdf route returns HTTP 200 + %PDF in the E2E stack; no regressions on the doc detail page
Self-assessment of the just-completed item via the iw-item-analyze skill
